rule.name: a + plural noun
rule.message: The article "a" usually does not precede a plural noun.

rule.name: I seen
rule.message: Did you mean "I saw" or "I have seen"?

rule.name: I done
rule.message: Did you mean "I did" or "I have done"?

rule.name: me and X went
rule.message: In subject position, prefer "I and …" or "… and I".

rule.name: any more vs anymore soft
rule.message: For quantity, "any more" (two words) is preferred in some guides.

rule.name: between you and I
rule.message: Did you mean "between you and me"?

rule.name: for you and I
rule.message: Did you mean "for you and me"?

rule.name: with you and I
rule.message: Did you mean "with you and me"?

rule.name: goes without saying soft
rule.message: Consider stating the point directly without the filler phrase.

rule.name: reason is because
rule.message: Prefer "reason is that" or drop "reason is".

rule.name: whether or not soft
rule.message: Often "whether to" is enough without "or not".

rule.name: principal/principle
rule.message: Did you mean "principle of the matter"?

rule.name: lay down rest
rule.message: Did you mean "lie down and rest"?

rule.name: nip in the butt
rule.message: Did you mean "nip it in the bud"?

rule.name: one in the same
rule.message: Did you mean "one and the same"?

rule.name: poured over
rule.message: Did you mean "pored over the document"?

rule.name: begs the question soft
rule.message: "Begs the question" means assumes the conclusion; for "raises the question" prefer that phrasing.

rule.name: in the vain of soft
rule.message: Did you mean "in the vein of"?

rule.name: pour over book soft
rule.message: Did you mean "pore over the book"?

rule.name: all intensive purposes soft xml
rule.message: Did you mean "all intents and purposes"?

rule.name: for all intensive alt soft
rule.message: Did you mean "for all intents and purposes"?

rule.name: in regards to soft
rule.message: Did you mean "in regard to" or "regarding"?

rule.name: with regards to soft
rule.message: Did you mean "with regard to" or "regarding"?

rule.name: all of the sudden soft
rule.message: Did you mean "all of a sudden"?

rule.name: literally figurative soft
rule.message: Figurative "literally" is often marked in picky style guides.

rule.name: in order to
rule.message: Consider simply "to" if shorter is better.

rule.name: different than
rule.message: In many style guides, prefer "different from".

rule.name: gotta soft style
rule.message: In formal writing, prefer "have to" or "got to".

rule.name: imma
rule.message: Did you mean "I'm going to" or "I'm a"?

rule.name: really really
rule.message: Possible accidental repetition of "really"; consider stronger wording or one "really".
